proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5 – Semantic correctness of coverage counters
; The function `runReport` aggregates proof statistics:
;   totalProven  = sum_{c ∈ contracts} |c.proven|
;   totalViolations = sum_{c ∈ contracts} |c.violations|
; Every contract’s `proven` and `violations` arrays have non‑negative length.
; We prove that `totalProven` and `totalViolations` can never be negative.
; ------------------------------------------------------------
; Model a concrete execution with two contracts (the argument works for any
; number of contracts because each contribution is >= 0).
(declare-const prov1 Int)          ; |contract1.proven|
(declare-const prov2 Int)          ; |contract2.proven|
(declare-const viol1 Int)          ; |contract1.violations|
(declare-const viol2 Int)          ; |contract2.violations|
(declare-const totalProven Int)
(declare-const totalViolations Int)

; ---- code transitions (assignments performed by the loop in runReport) ----
(assert (>= prov1 0))              ; array length cannot be negative
(assert (>= prov2 0))
(assert (>= viol1 0))
(assert (>= viol2 0))

; totalProven = prov1 + prov2
(assert (= totalProven (+ prov1 prov2)))
; totalViolations = viol1 + viol2
(assert (= totalViolations (+ viol1 viol2)))

; ---- negation of the property we want to prove (that a total is negative) ----
(assert (< totalProven 0))          ; would mean a negative proven count
(assert (< totalViolations 0))      ; would mean a negative violation count

; If the program is correct, the above constraints are UNSAT
(check-sat)
